tasks.title: Add OAuth Login Page
tasks.description: Create `pages/login.tsx` with a 'Sign in with Google' button that links to `/api/auth/google`. Use Next.js 14 App Router. Patterns: see `pages/signup.tsx` for the button and layout pattern. Out of scope: do not implement the OAuth redirect or callback logic — that is a separate task. Verify: confirm `pages/login.tsx` exists; the 'Sign in with Google' button is present and its href is `/api/auth/google`; all imports resolve to existing files.

tasks.title: Handle OAuth Callback
tasks.description: Before implementing, read `lib/auth/` and `models/user.ts` to understand the existing session and user persistence patterns. Add an API route at `pages/api/auth/callback.ts` that receives the OAuth code, exchanges it for a token using the existing OAuth client in `lib/auth/oauth.ts`, and stores the session in an httpOnly cookie. Use the existing `User` model in `models/user.ts` to persist the user record. Patterns: see `pages/api/auth/session.ts` for the cookie-setting pattern. Out of scope: do not implement the login page or the sign-out route — those are separate tasks. Verify: confirm `pages/api/auth/callback.ts` exists; the OAuth token exchange uses `lib/auth/oauth.ts`; the session is stored in an httpOnly cookie; the `User` model from `models/user.ts` is used to persist the record.
